concerns.text: SC-002 requires measuring the 'performance drop' by comparing intra-family vs inter-family splits. T021 implements the 'inter-family generalization test' but there is no corresponding task to explicitly generate and report the 'intra-family' baseline metrics required to calculate the specific 'drop' mentioned in the success criterion.

FR-005 and US-3 Acceptance Scenario 1 require outputting a 'single ranked list' combining SHAP and permutation importance. T023 and T024 implement the calculations, but T027 (report generation) does not explicitly state it must merge these into a single ranked list, risking fragmented output that fails the acceptance criteria.

The Analyze Report flagged a critical contradiction: the project title in spec/plan/tasks still reads 'First-Principles Calculations' despite reviewer mandates to use 'Structure-Only Surrogate Model'. While T029-T031 address terminology in code comments, there is no task to update the actual artifact titles in spec.md, plan.md, and tasks.md to resolve the contradiction.

FR-007 and SC-004 require enforcing a 6h/7GB compute budget. T008 creates 'memory_utils.py' for chunked processing, but there is no task to implement the actual 'sampling strategy' or 'data volume verification' mentioned in the plan's 'Scale/Scope' section (which defers sample size). Without a task to define the sampling logic or verify the dataset size against the 7GB limit, the constraint cannot be satisfied.

FR-001 requires downloading from 'Materials Project and AFLOW APIs'. The Constitution Principle I mandates fetching from the 'same canonical source on every run' for reproducibility. T009 implements fetching from both APIs, but there is no task to implement the 'unified source' logic or fallback mechanism required to satisfy the Constitution's reproducibility constraint if one API fails or rate-limits.
